goal: compose[T, U, V](source, right, x) = source(right(x))
proof: function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T1 -> T2, x1: T0 -> T1, x2: T0) { compose[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = x0(x1(x2)) }[T, U, V](source, right, x)

goal: source(right(x)) = compose[U, T, V](target, inverse_fn[T, U](right), right(x))
compose[U, T, V](target, inverse_fn[T, U](right), right(x)) = target(inverse_fn[T, U](right, right(x)))
proof: function[T0, T1, T2](x0: T1 -> T2, x1: T0 -> T1, x2: T0) { compose[T0, T1, T2](x0, x1, x2) = x0(x1(x2)) }[U, T, V](target, inverse_fn[T, U](right), right(x))

goal: inverse_fn[T, U](right, right(x)) = x
target(inverse_fn[T, U](right, right(x))) = target(x)
precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, source, x) = target(x)
precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, source) = target

proof: function(x0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x0) != target }(source)

goal: is_surjective_fn[U -> V, T -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T1 -> T0) { forall(x1: T0) { exists(k0: T1) { x0(k0) = x1 } } = is_surjective_fn[T1, T0](x0) }[T -> V, U -> V](precompose_fn[T, U, V](right))
let w0: T -> V satisfy { forall(x0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x0) != w0 } }
function(x0: T -> V) { exists(k0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, k0) = x0 } }(w0)
let w1: U -> V satisfy { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w1) = w0 }
let w2(arg0: T -> V) -> w2_result: U -> V satisfy { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w2_result) = arg0 }
function(x0: T -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, w2(x0)) = x0 }(w0)
(forall(x1: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x1) != w0 } = true)
function(x0: U -> V) { precompose_fn[T, U, V](right, x0) != w0 }(w2(w0))

let w0: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { exists(k0: Bijection[T, U]) { compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, k0) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w0) and k0 != w0 } }
let w1: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w0) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w1) and w0 != w1 }
let w2: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w0) = compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, w2) and w0 != w2 }
function(x0: Bijection[T, U], x1: Bijection[T, U]) { compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, x0) != compose_bijection[T, U, V](e, x1) or x0 = x1 }(w0, w2)

let w1: Bijection[T, U] satisfy { inverse_bijection[T, U](w0) = inverse_bijection[T, U](w1) and w0 != w1 }

goal: is_surjective_fn[Bijection[T, U], Bijection[U, T]](inverse_bijection[T, U])

function[T0: Inhabited, T1: Inhabited](x0: Bijection[T1, T0]) { inverse_bijection[T0, T1](inverse_bijection[T1, T0](x0)) = x0 }[T, U](w0)
(forall(x1: Bijection[T, U]) { inverse_bijection[T, U](x1) != w0 } = true)
function(x0: Bijection[T, U]) { inverse_bijection[T, U](x0) != w0 }(inverse_bijection[U, T](w0))
